name: MITRE.LIN.T1222.RULE File and Directory Permissions Modification
notes:
The following auditd rules should be enabled:

-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chmod -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S chown -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchmod -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchmodat -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchown -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fchownat -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fremovexattr -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S fsetxattr -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lchown -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lremovexattr -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S lsetxattr -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S removexattr -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod
-a always,exit -F arch=b64 -S setxattr -F auid!=4294967295 -k siem-perm-mod

Get more Linux MITRE rules: https://www.scnsoft.com/services/security/siem/linux-mitre-attack-rules
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-perm-mod
when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
TD_list: APPLY MITRE.LIN.T1222.RULE File and Directory Permissions Modification on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Linux OS
AND when the event matches Linux Auditd Key (custom) is any of siem-perm-mod
AND NOT when any of Linux UID (custom) are contained in any of MITRE: Linux Users - AlphaNumeric (Ignore Case)
RED_list: Event name: MITRE.LIN.T1222.RULE File and Directory Permissions Modification

Event description: Adversaries may modify file or directory permissions/attributes to evade intended DACLs. Modifications may include changing specific access rights, which may require taking ownership of a file or directory and/or elevated permissions such as Administrator/root depending on the file or directory's existing permissions to enable malicious activity such as modifying, replacing, or deleting specific files/directories.
See more on: https://attack.mitre.org/techniques/T1222/
Force the dispatched event to create a NEW offense, select the offense using Source IP
Include detected events by "Source IP" from this point forward, in the offense, for 0 second(s)
This information should contribute to the name of the associated offense(s)
